sources.writer_profile: Alfonso Campos, writing as El Gocho Gourmet, is a Granada-born food critic and hospitality professional who trained at the city's Escuela de Hostelería Hurtado de Mendoza and has worked in several of Granada's most respected restaurants, including Carmen de Aben Humeya and Restaurante Arriaga. He is the creator of the El Gocho Gourmet restaurant guide to Granada — now in its second edition with Ayuntamiento backing — and writes a regular gastronomy column for GranadaDigital, the city's leading independent digital newspaper. His editorial focus centres on authentic, locally rooted establishments rather than tourist-facing venues.

James Dimitri is an independent food and travel writer who visited Granada in February 2025 and documented the city's distinctive free-tapa bar culture with first-hand recommendations. His writing focuses on the authentic everyday dining experience — in particular, the tapas bar tradition that distinguishes Granada from other Spanish cities. His guide reflects the perspective of a knowledgeable, independently minded visitor looking for genuine local bars rather than tourist-facing restaurants.

Lauren Aloise is an American food writer who studied in Granada and has returned to the city more than twenty times, building deep personal knowledge of its dining scene. She writes the Spanish Sabores food blog and is also co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in several Spanish cities. ⚠ coi: her commercial food tour interests should be noted when weighing her restaurant recommendations.
sources.commercial_conflict_note: Lauren Aloise is co-founder of Devour Tours, a commercial food tour operator active in Spain. ⚠ coi

sources.writer_profile: Fanny writes for Andalucia Lovers, part of the Region Lovers network of destination travel sites, which publishes first-hand restaurant reviews with no sponsoring. Her Granada restaurant guide, published in February 2025, focuses on the historic centre and highlights a mix of contemporary and traditional dining options, based on actual visits.

restaurants.name: Bar Avila Tapas
restaurants.signature_dish: Jamón asado (slow roast pork); pollo asado; croquetas
restaurants.quote: Jamón asado (slow roast pork) — crispy on the outside, moist in the middle. Also: croquetas, bocadillos, and pollo asado with salt, herbs, and cooking juices. No reservations taken.

restaurants.name: Bar Los Diamantes
restaurants.quote: Fried fish specialties — ensaladilla rusa, calamares, gambas, and razor clams — at this legendary seafood bar. Generous free tapa with each drink at the original Calle Navas location.

restaurants.name: Bodegas Castañeda
restaurants.signature_dish: Rabo de toro; house cocktail calicasas
restaurants.quote: Tortilla and potato salad, rabo de toro (oxtail stew with peppers and potatoes), jamón, and the house calicasas cocktail — a Granada institution since 1927.

restaurants.name: Café 4 Gatos
restaurants.signature_dish: Toast with tomato and manchego; fresh orange juice
restaurants.quote: Toast with tomato, manchego, jamón, coffee, and fresh orange juice — the go-to Albaicín breakfast stop. Enormous and delicious toasts in a hidden placeta.

restaurants.name: Betula Nana
restaurants.quote: Ajo blanco — cold almond-garlic soup with anchovies — at this 18-cover contemporary seafood gastrobar near the Monastery of San Jerónimo. Reservations via WhatsApp only; closed Mondays.

restaurants.name: Casa de Vinos La Brujidera
restaurants.signature_dish: Local wines; tapas improving with each round
restaurants.quote: Local wines served with tapas that improve in quality and generosity as the evening progresses — a reliable wine-bar stop in the city centre.

restaurants.name: El Mercader
restaurants.signature_dish: Lebanese-inspired lamb with mint and couscous; spiced orange cake with white chocolate
restaurants.quote: Lebanese-inspired lamb subtly flavoured with mint, served with couscous and white yogurt; followed by a spiced orange cake with white chocolate sauce — a creative fusion restaurant five minutes from the Cathedral.

restaurants.name: Kiosko Las Titas
restaurants.quote: Art Nouveau style establishment facing the Genil River, offering Mediterranean tradition with Granada touches. Suitable for quiet afternoon drinks and evening dining, with a varied menu of quality ingredients designed for sharing.

restaurants.name: La Barra de San Remo
restaurants.quote: The newer incarnation of the classic San Remo, run by Aitor Pozuelo — characterised by neighbourhood tradition, flavour, and familiarity. Informal and welcoming, ideal for extended afternoons sharing tapas and good conversation.

restaurants.name: La Botillería
restaurants.signature_dish: Carrillada (pork cheeks in plum and sherry sauce); homemade desserts
restaurants.quote: Good wine selection, carrillada (pork cheeks in plum and sherry sauce) tapas, and homemade desserts at this Realejo-border restaurant on Calle Varela.

restaurants.name: La Trastienda
restaurants.signature_dish: Cured meats and artisanal cheeses paired with wine
restaurants.quote: Cured meats, artisanal cheeses, and carefully selected wines — a warm wine-and-charcuterie spot on the Plaza de Cuchilleros in the Realejo.

restaurants.name: Perromedio
restaurants.quote: Visiting Perromedio is not just going out to eat: it is a gastronomic experience that connects past and present. The restaurant combines the authenticity of recipes like rabo de toro — a dish whose meat dissolves in the mouth, impregnated with sofrito, red wine, and spices — with careful service and a privileged setting in the city centre.

restaurants.name: Pimienta Rosa
restaurants.signature_dish: Arroz seco de presa ibérica; fried eggplant with honey; white chocolate and cauliflower croquettes
restaurants.quote: Arroz seco de presa ibérica — Andalusian rice with Iberian pork and artichokes — fried eggplant with honey, and white chocolate and cauliflower croquettes. Traditional Granada flavours with contemporary technique.

restaurants.name: Taberna Catavinos
restaurants.quote: Baby squid and generous raciones at this neighbourhood Albayzín taberna — one of Granada's nine new Repsol Solete winners in 2024.

restaurants.name: Tajine Elvira
restaurants.quote: Couscous, fragrant tagines, and pastela at this Albayzín Moroccan restaurant — one of the most popular dining spots in the neighbourhood among locals.
